Plot variable importance via bootstrap stability selection

plot.importance.Rd

Provides a stability-based assessment of variable importance by repeatedly re-running selection on the fitted BRIER model using bootstrap resamples of the validation set as the held-out data. For each replication, BRIERi.selection is re-run on the bootstrap subsample to pick a new (\(\eta^*\), \(\lambda^*\)); the procedure then records which predictors enter the model (have nonzero coefficient) at that choice via coef.BRIER. Selection frequencies are summarised across all replications.

Usage

# S3 method for class 'importance'
plot(
  object,
  X,
  covar.data,
  criteria = c("gaussian.mspe", "gaussian.rsq", "binomial.dev", "binomial.mcfrsq",
    "binomial.tjursq", "binomial.auc", "poisson.dev"),
  pheno.name = "y",
  adjust.covar = NULL,
  standardize.data = FALSE,
  n.top = 20L,
  replications = 100L,
  bootstrap.size = NULL,
  seed = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

object

An object of class "BRIER" from BRIERi, BRIERs, or BRIERfull.

X

A numeric matrix of validation-set predictors (n_val x p).

covar.data

A vector, matrix, or data.frame of validation-set outcomes and (optionally) confounders. Vectors are treated as the phenotype and given column name pheno.name.

criteria

A string specifying the validation criterion passed to BRIERi.selection on each bootstrap subsample. One of: "gaussian.mspe", "gaussian.rsq", "binomial.dev", "binomial.mcfrsq", "binomial.tjursq", "binomial.auc", "poisson.dev".

pheno.name

Character. Column name of the trait of interest in covar.data. Defaults to "y".

adjust.covar

Optional character vector of confounder column names. Ignored with a warning for binomial and Poisson criteria.

standardize.data

Logical. If TRUE, the design matrix X is standardized per bootstrap subsample; the phenotype is also standardized for Gaussian criteria.

n.top

Integer. Number of top predictors to display, ranked by selection frequency. Defaults to 20.

replications

Integer. Number of bootstrap replications. Defaults to 100.

bootstrap.size

Integer. Sample size for each bootstrap subsample. Defaults to floor(0.8 * nrow(X)).

seed

Optional integer seed for reproducible bootstrap sampling.

...

Unused; present for S3 method compatibility.

Value

A list with the following elements:

plot

A ggplot bar plot of the top n.top predictors by selection frequency.

summary.df

A data.frame with one row per predictor (intercept excluded), containing the variable name, selection count, and selection frequency, sorted in descending order of frequency.

selection.mat

A logical matrix (replications x p) where each row indicates which predictors were selected (nonzero coefficient) in that replication.

Details

This works for all three model classes (BRIERi, BRIERs, BRIERfull) because they share the "BRIER" class and BRIERi.selection dispatches uniformly. It re-uses the existing fit and only re-runs lambda/eta selection on each bootstrap subsample - it does not refit BRIER. Standardization of X, when requested, is applied per bootstrap subsample to avoid information leakage from out-of-subsample observations. The intercept (when present in the fitted coefficient matrix) is automatically excluded from the importance summary, since it is not a predictor of interest.

The resulting plot displays the top n.top predictors ranked by selection frequency. High-frequency predictors indicate stable, reproducible signals; low-frequency predictors may reflect noise or dataset-specific fluctuations.

See also

plot.eta, plot.box, BRIERi.selection, coef.BRIER

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
fit <- BRIERi(
  X, y, family = "gaussian",
  eta.list = list(c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4)),
  beta.external = beta.external
)
sel <- BRIERi.selection(fit, criteria = "BIC")

out <- plot.importance(
  sel, X = X_validation, covar.data = pheno_validation,
  criteria = "gaussian.mspe",
  pheno.name = "trait", adjust.covar = c("age", "sex"),
  n.top = 10, replications = 100, seed = 1
)
out$plot
head(out$summary.df, 10)
} # }
